**St Benedict's Church, Bordesley** is a [Church of England parish church](/wiki/Church_of_England_parish_church "Church of England parish church") in Hob Moor Road,
[Bordesley](/wiki/Bordesley,_West_Midlands "Bordesley, West Midlands"), [West Midlands](/wiki/West_Midlands_(county) "West Midlands (county)"), [England](/wiki/England "England"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) about 2+1⁄2 miles (4 km) east of [Birmingham](/wiki/Birmingham "Birmingham") city centre. It is an early 20th-century church in [Byzantine Revival](/wiki/Byzantine_Revival_architecture "Byzantine Revival architecture") style[[2]](#cite_note-2) and is [Grade II listed](/wiki/Listed_building#Categories_of_listed_building "Listed building").

## History and description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Bordesley&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History and description")]

[St Oswald's Church, Small Heath](/wiki/St_Oswald%27s_Church,_Small_Heath "St Oswald's Church, Small Heath") established a mission church in the area in 1898. It was a temporary iron building.[[3]](#cite_note-TNA-3)

The architects Nicol and Nicol of Birmingham designed the present church as its permanent replacement. It was built in 1909[[4]](#cite_note-NHLE-4) and consecrated on 30 April 1910.[[3]](#cite_note-TNA-3) It is in Byzantine Revival style, built of red brick with [red sandstone](/wiki/New_Red_Sandstone "New Red Sandstone") dressings. The [nave](/wiki/Nave "Nave") is flanked by north and south [aisles](/wiki/Aisle#Church_architecture "Aisle"). Between them are five-[bay](/wiki/Bay_(architecture) "Bay (architecture)") [arcades](/wiki/Arcade_(architecture) "Arcade (architecture)") with round-headed arches on sandstone [piers](/wiki/Pier_(architecture) "Pier (architecture)"). At the west end of the north aisle is a [pedimented](/wiki/Pediment "Pediment") porch. A statue of [Benedict of Nursia](/wiki/Benedict_of_Nursia "Benedict of Nursia"), the church's dedicatee, stands in a niche above its door.[[4]](#cite_note-NHLE-4)

At the east end of the church the [chancel](/wiki/Chancel "Chancel") has an [apse](/wiki/Apse "Apse"). Inside it is a Byzantine-style painting of the apse representing [Christ in Majesty](/wiki/Christ_in_Majesty "Christ in Majesty"), with angels, and saints in arcading, below. It was painted by [Henry Holiday](/wiki/Henry_Holiday "Henry Holiday") between 1912 and 1919.[[4]](#cite_note-NHLE-4)

The church was made a Grade II listed building in July 1982.[[4]](#cite_note-NHLE-4) Its parish archives were deposited with [Birmingham Central Library](/wiki/Birmingham_Central_Library "Birmingham Central Library") in February 1998 and are now with its successor, the [Library of Birmingham](/wiki/Library_of_Birmingham "Library of Birmingham").[[3]](#cite_note-TNA-3)

## Organ

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Bordesley&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Organ")]

St Benedict's has an organ in the north aisle of the chancel. It that was built by CH Windridge of Birmingham. It pre-dates the church, having been built in 1894–95. It was modified about 1920 by Conacher of [Sheffield](/wiki/Sheffield "Sheffield"). It has three [manuals](/wiki/Manual_(music) "Manual (music)"), one set of pedals and 22 [stops](/wiki/Organ_stop "Organ stop").
[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Vicarage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Bordesley&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Vicarage")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3b/St_Benedicts_Vicarage_Birmingham_-_Andy_Mabbett_-_2016-12-02_-_06.jpg/250px-St_Benedicts_Vicarage_Birmingham_-_Andy_Mabbett_-_2016-12-02_-_06.jpg)](/wiki/File:St_Benedicts_Vicarage_Birmingham_-_Andy_Mabbett_-_2016-12-02_-_06.jpg)

St Benedict's Vicarage

St Benedict's [vicarage](/wiki/Clergy_house "Clergy house") is next door to the church in Hob Moor Road. Like the church it was designed by Nicol and Nicol. It was built in 1911–12. [English Heritage](/wiki/English_Heritage "English Heritage") made it a Grade II listed building in 1997.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
